[PDF_PAGE 63 | score=12] Golem, General CLIMATE TERRAIN: FREQUENCY: rare ORGANIZATION: Solitary ACTIVITY CYCLE: DIET: Nil INTELLIGENCE: Non- (0) TREASURE: Nil ALIGNMENT: Neutral Golems are magically created automatons of great power. The construction of one involves mighty magics and elemental forces_ Background: Golems predate any known writings. The wizard who discovered the process, if indeed there was only one 1S not known Some of the rediscoverers have written down their se - crets in various arcane manuals, enchanted to aid the reader in constructing the golem . It is thought that the first golem created was ] flesh possibly an accident of some great wizard ex- perimenting with reanima human bodies. Flesh gole

It can smell scrolls, maps, arcane tomes and books at a distance of 60 feet_ Normally a dull gray color; a bookworm's chameleon-like abil- When an adventurer is careless e
[PDF_PAGE 99 | score=5] Mummy CLIMATETERRAIN: Desert subterranean FREQUENCY: Rare ORGANIZATION: Pack ACTIITY CYCLE: Night DIET: None INTELLIGENCE: Low (5-7) TREASURE: P (D) ALIGNMENT: Lawful evil NO:APPEARING: 2-8 (2d4) ARMOR CLASS: MOVEMENT: HIT DICE: 6 + 3 THACO: 13 NO. OF ATTACKS: DAMAGEATTACK: 1-12 SPECIAL ATTACKS: Fear, disease SPECIAL DEFENSES: See below MAGIC RESISTANCE: Nil SIZE: M (6') MORALE: Champion (15) XP VALUE: 3,000 Mummies are corpses native to desert areas, where the dead are entombed by a process known as mummification. When their tombs are disturbed the corpses become animated into a weird unlife state, whose unholy hatred of life causes them to attack liv - mies are corpses without magical prop
[PDF_PAGE 30 | score=4] Dragons Dragons are an ancient; winged reptilian race They are known and feared for their size, physical prowess, and magical abilities. The 10 sub- species of dragons are identified by the color of their scales. Each sub- species has 12 age categories: The oldest dragons are among the most powerful creatures in the world. Dragons range in size from several feet upon hatching to more than 100 feet after have attained the status of great wyrm. The exact size varies according to age and subspecies A dragon $ wing span is about to its body length and fully 15-20 % of a dragon s body length is its neck; Dragons, especially older ones are generally solitary due to necessity and preference. They d
